title: Caretaker stage B — embedding-based L3 dedup (full mode)
current_state: T-125a covers derived recompute. Near-identical L3 entries ('User likes coffee' / 'User loves coffee') still accumulate independently.
structural_problem: Without dedup, L3 grows redundant; search noise increases; the subway-order class of recall confusion gets worse over time.
target_state: caretaker.full() = caretaker.lite() + embedding dedup pass. Walks active L3; for each entry, finds neighbours with cosine > 0.92; merges keeping highest-importance; sets superseded_by on the rest. Runs session-exit + daily cron (NOT per-bubble — too expensive).
design_notes: Cosine threshold: 0.92 (more conservative than recall's 0.85 — fewer false merges)
Merge policy: keep highest importance; if tie, keep newest created_at
Loser entries set superseded_by = winner_id; remain in DB for audit
Batch by category (only compare entries in same category) to bound O(N²) cost
Bound: max 500 entries scanned per run; resume from last_processed_id
success_criteria: Two near-identical writes ('User likes coffee' / 'User loves coffee') merged after full pass
Higher-importance entry wins; loser has superseded_by set
Full mode completes in <30s on 1000-row L3
risk_notes: False merge risk: distinct meanings collapsed. Mitigation: same-category requirement + 0.92 threshold + dry-run flag for first deploy.
migration_plan: 3. Wire as session-exit step + daily cron.
